channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-Israel Conflict in Iran Moves to Attrition with Economic and Regional Impacts
item.description: On the fifth day of the US-Israel war against Iran, the conflict has entered a phase of attrition rather than decisive military victory. The coalition has gained tactical air superiority, significantl...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Hazing Death of Maritime Student in Cavite
item.description: Authorities in Cavite are investigating the death of 19-year-old freshman maritime student Mark Kenneth Alcedo, who reportedly died due to severe hazing. Police identified 21 individuals as involved, ...

item.title: Lamine Yamal’s First Career Hat-trick Leads Barcelona To 4-1 Victory Over Villarreal
item.description: Barcelona young star Lamine Yamal shine brightly as he score the first hat-trick of him professional career to help Barcelona defeat Villarreal 4-1 and remain top of the La Liga table. The 18-year-old...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif Calls In-Camera Briefing for Parliamentary Leaders on Pakistan-Afghanistan Border Tensions and Middle East Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has invited parliamentary leaders and heads of political parties to an in-camera briefing scheduled for Wednesday at 11:30am to address pressing domestic and regional sec...

item.title: The emergence of veavage as the latest red carpet trend
item.description: The latest red carpet trend is the 'veavage', a deep V-shaped neckline plunging to the waist, now seen on stars like Gwyneth Paltrow, Kristen Bell, Jenna Ortega, and Zendaya. Unlike traditional cleava...

item.title: Keir Starmer questions US strategy on Iran and defends UK's cautious approach
item.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has openly criticised former US President Donald Trump, asserting that he does not possess a 'viable, thought-through plan' regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran. T...

item.title: Bank of Canada Governor Warns of Market Risks Amid Iran Conflict and Growing Non-Bank Lending
item.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem addressed the Global Risk Institute in Toronto, highlighting heightened vulnerabilities in the financial system amid global uncertainty. He pointed to the recent U...

item.title: Large VA Study Suggests GLP-1 Medications Like Ozempic May Reduce Risk of Multiple Substance Use Disorders and Related Harms
item.description: A new observational study analyzing electronic health records from over 600,000 U.S. veterans with type 2 diabetes, published in The BMJ, indicates that GLP-1 receptor agonist medications—such as sema...

item.title: New treatment offers hope for children with Dravet syndrome
item.description: A new drug, zorevunersen, is showing remarkable results for children with Dravet syndrome, a severe form of epilepsy affecting approximately one in 15,000 babies. The condition can trigger dozens of d...

item.title: Illegal Skin Lightening Creams with Harmful Substances Like Mercury and Hydroquinone Sold in UK Shops
item.description: In the United Kingdom, skin lightening creams are being sold illegally in high street shops, including butchers and specialist food stores. These products often contain banned and dangerous ingredient...

item.title: Government trains procurement officers on sustainable supply chain management
item.description: The Ministry of Finance in Tanzania has organized a short-term training for Government Procurement and Supply Officers to boost their professional knowledge and practical skills in sustainable supply ...

item.title: Harry Styles reflects on the difficulty of grieving Liam Payne under public scrutiny
item.description: British pop star Harry Styles has spoken openly about the emotional difficulty he faced after the death of his former One Direction bandmate Liam Payne, explaining that he found it challenging to grie...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Pays Tribute to Mosiuoa Lekota, COPE Founder and Former Minister
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has expressed deep sorrow over the passing of Mosiuoa Gerard Patrick Lekota, the former Premier of the Free State, Minister of Defence, and founding leader of the Congress of...

item.title: Supreme Court reconstitutes SJC, JCP and Practice and Procedure Committee after 27th Constitutional Amendment
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has announced the reconstitution of three major judicial and constitutional bodies following the implementation of the 27th Constitutional Amendment. According...

item.title: Trump says he helped prevent escalation between Pakistan and India
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday stated that he has prevented eight wars, including one between Pakistan and India which was reportedly on the verge of restarting. Speaking at a joint press conf...

item.title: Saskatchewan Gas Prices Rise Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Drivers in Saskatchewan are facing higher gas prices due to increased tensions in the Middle East. Devan Mescall, an accounting professor at the University of Saskatchewan, explained that around 30 pe...

item.title: UK Government Maintains Cautious Approach to Iran Amid US Pressure
item.description: During Prime Minister's Questions, Sir Keir Starmer defended the UK government's response to escalating tensions in Iran, stressing that protecting British nationals remains the top priority. The disc...

item.title: Grenade thrown at Lamitan fast food outlet injures one worker
item.description: In Lamitan, Basilan, a grenade attack targeted a newly constructed fast food branch, injuring a 22-year-old worker named Rebel Masulog. The incident happened around 7:30 p.m. on Tuesday and was report...

item.title: US Financial Sector Heightens Cybersecurity Measures Amid Rising Iran Tensions
item.description: Amid the rising tensions due to the US military action in Iran, American banks and financial institutions have stepped up their cybersecurity measures to prevent potential attacks. Following the assas...

item.title: Ondo Pastors Allege Extortion and Threats By Police Over Planned Crusade
item.description: Some pastors for Ondo State, including Olarewaju Fadahunsi, Gbadura Marvelous, Arijesulola Kayode, Tijani, and Kolade, don claim say dem experience serious wahala from police under Commissioner Adebow...
